title: The Necromancer
timelinePosition: Runs parallel to Chapter 11. Opens after Jaina's descent at the end of Chapter 10 and ends at the instant Sylvanas's approach reaches the deep levels of Scholomance.
openLocation: Kel'Thuzad's inner sanctum beneath Scholomance, deep below Caer Darrow.
transport: On foot through the lower crypt levels before open; seated inside the sanctum at chapter start.
immediateObjective: Extract the missing animation-trigger mechanism, understand the architecture behind the plague, and survive the conversation without mistaking comprehension for surrender.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
Keep Kel'Thuzad calm, human, and scholarly rather than theatrical.
Keep Sylvanas fully off-page until the final beat; her absence should register as missing epistemic friction, not recap.
No Jaina conversion, no rescue already inside the room, and no repair of the split partnership in this chapter.

povKnowsAtOpen: She reached the lower levels alone by using Jandice's scholarly vanity as the access vector.
The Barov estate and Scholomance are live plague-support infrastructure, not rumor or abandoned ruins.
Her Chapter 9 field read yielded binding sequences but not the animation trigger; the missing mechanism is why she kept descending.
She already knows the treated grain is only the carrier layer and that the body participates in the rest, but not how that participation resolves into undead continuity.
Jandice's theory named resonance as the key concept completing the Chapter 9 gap.
The Kirin Tor is too slow to help at the pace this crisis is moving.
Sylvanas and Cyndia took the Arthas thread in Chapter 9, so Jaina is operating without tactical backup.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Kel'Thuzad wants to recruit her, test her, or simply teach her.
What sits above Kel'Thuzad in the chain of command.
Whether Sylvanas is still far south or already turning north.
How she leaves with the data if the conversation goes bad.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No Jaina agreement or moral conversion.
No theatrical villain register from Kel'Thuzad.
No rescue already inside the room.
No resolution of the old seller / repeated-farewell clue.
No repair of the split partnership before Chapter 13.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Jaina leaves the conversation with the complete resonance model and animation trigger, including the distinction between low-fidelity village preservation and high-fidelity preserved selfhood, plus the realization that necromancy is a continuous extension of orthodox arcane theory rather than a disconnected profane system.
She now knows, from Kel'Thuzad's own disclosures, that the plague serves the Lich King and that something called the Burning Legion sits above that chain.
Kel'Thuzad remains alive in the sanctum while Jandice remains above it.
Sylvanas is descending toward the room, setting the Chapter 13 tableau.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina can finally get the full answer, she can hold the room on intellect alone.
pov.surfaceObjective: Get the complete mechanism and the real scale of the threat from the one man who can explain it cleanly.
pov.pressureSource: Kel'Thuzad is not a ranting monster. He is lucid, rigorous, and speaking in the language Jaina was trained to love.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps leaning toward the explanation itself, as if a clear theorem can stay morally separate from what it is for.
pov.chapterTest: The answer arrives through a scholar whose mind feels legible to her, and the intellectual fit keeps pulling her forward faster than judgment does.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep treating the mechanism as pure answer, or confront what the answer serves and what line it crossed to become this elegant.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina gets the full mechanism, but loses the safety of believing intellect is its own moral guardrail.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 13 opens on aftermath Sylvanas did not witness, with Jaina intellectually and morally scorched by what she learned.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Living academic endpoint, plague architect, and the scholar who can finally speak to Jaina at full speed.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: The danger should look like overt monstrosity, madness, or theatrical villainy.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: The danger is that Kel'Thuzad is lucid, rigorous, intellectually legible to her, and working from extensions of the same magical schooling she received.
throughPov.interactionRule: Keep him calm, human, and scholarly. The threat is the clarity.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Jaina alone in the room, with no Sylvanas friction on the thinking.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The missing partnership becomes the chapter's engine. Every escalation survives because no one is there to price the room, cut the lecture short, or force the question of what the theory does to actual people.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Keep Sylvanas fully off-page until the final beat. Her absence should land through missing checks, not recap.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is exposure and threshold, not conversion or agreement.

entries.rule: Alive, calm, scholarly, and still fully human at this point. He is not yet a lich and must not sound like a fantasy prosecutor or a ranting villain.
Present only in the opening threshold handoff. She does not sit in on the main conversation.
Off-page on the parallel northbound approach until the final beat. They do not enter the sanctum during this chapter.
Present only through shared training, shared notation, and the personal wound Kel'Thuzad opens.
No payoff here. Keep it absent.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 13 opens on Sylvanas entering this room and misreading aftermath she did not witness.

factsLockedForChapter13: Jaina has the complete resonance model, animation trigger mechanism, and the distinction between low-fidelity village preservation and high-fidelity preserved selfhood.
Jaina now understands that Dalaran banned necromancy on ethical and civic grounds rather than because the underlying magical theory failed.
Jaina now knows, from Kel'Thuzad's own disclosures, that the plague serves the Lich King and that a force called the Burning Legion sits above that chain.
Jaina had the moment of comprehension and cannot unknow it.
Kel'Thuzad is alive, calm, seated, and still in full control of the room at chapter close.
Jandice was dismissed earlier and is above the sanctum, not inside the final exchange.
Sylvanas is already in the building and descending toward the room; Cyndia remains on the Caer Darrow approach and outside the sanctum.
The old seller / repeated-farewell clue remains completely unresolved by this thread.
factsNotYetEarned: Any correct reading of Jaina's face and posture by Sylvanas.
Any repair of the split partnership.
Any fuller exposition or outside confirmation of the Burning Legion beyond Kel'Thuzad's brief namedrop here.
Any tribunal or institutional accounting for what Jaina learned here.

scenes.title: The Sanctum
scenes.sceneFunction: Establish that the deepest room in Scholomance is an office, not a lair, and let Kel'Thuzad complete the missing mechanism in theorem-and-hypothesis language open enough that Jaina's mind engages before her guard can close.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina is already inside the sanctum on Kel'Thuzad's permission; she has not yet learned what kind of room gives the answer.
scenes.arcPressure: The answer arrives in a room built to feel like Jaina's own education turned inside out.

enrichment.detail: Canon Caer Darrow and Scholomance grounding supports an island fortress above a crypt-academy: old stone, preservative reek, still lake air, and academic spaces nested inside necromantic infrastructure rather than a theatrical dungeon. Keeping the sanctum office-like rather than lair-like aligns the chapter with both Scholomance's converted-school identity and Kel'Thuzad's scholar profile.

beats.goal: Recover footing inside Kel'Thuzad's sanctum and read what kind of encounter this already is.
beats.conflict: Jaina is already seated inside the room on someone else's permission, Jandice is being dismissed at the threshold, and everything in sight says scholar rather than monster: organized desk, notation boards, specimen jars, and Antonidas's notation system everywhere.

outcome.summary: Jaina is already in the room that contains the answer, but the room is built to feel like her own education turned inside out.

beats.valueShift: descent into enemy ground -> intimate intellectual danger
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad's sanctum is a working study, not a theatrical throne room.
Jandice's authority has a ceiling, and that ceiling is Kel'Thuzad.
The scholarship in the room is recognizably Kirin Tor-shaped even where the subject matter is necromantic.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Why Kel'Thuzad is willing to receive her at all.
Whether the conversation is trap, test, or genuine exchange.
sensoryAnchors: cold dry air
preservative compounds
green luminescent fungi
books and chalkboards under submerged light
Antonidas-style margin habits
beats.transitionIntent: scholar-recognition: once the room and Kel'Thuzad's register are legible, he can give her exactly what she came for and make the danger worse.
beats.verifierModification: Shifted the opening beat from fresh arrival to already-seated threshold handoff so chapter12 does not replay chapter10's descent.
beats.verifierNotes: Chapter 10 already established Jandice as the access vector and Scholomance as a working institution; do not restage this as first-contact gothic spectacle or give Jandice more authority than Kel'Thuzad.

beats.goal: Obtain the missing animation-trigger mechanism from the incomplete Chapter 9 field data.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad does not force the answer from her; he teaches it, and the teaching is accurate enough that Jaina's analytical mind engages before she can reject the source.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets the missing mechanism, but getting it means meeting Kel'Thuzad on shared intellectual ground.

beats.valueShift: intimate intellectual danger -> rigorous engagement with the mechanism
newOnPageInformation: The plague's animation model is resonance-based rather than force-based.
The treated grain carries only the outer shell of the plague, not the full waking mechanism.
Conditioned tissue retains patterned potential that can be amplified instead of externally commanded.
The body is the answering chamber that completes what the carrier started.
Most village dead preserve only low-fidelity pattern enough for movement and answerability; preserving full selfhood requires an additional deliberate layer.

The Cult's field necromancers are mostly acolyte conductors taught command signals; they can seize and harden the crude choir into direct compliance without creating that underlying self-mustering from nothing.
Jaina's incomplete Chapter 9 data now has a complete theoretical home.
Kel'Thuzad frames necromancy as an extension of accepted arcane work on persistence, binding, and sympathetic carryover rather than as a separate profane system.
Jaina has nobody in the room who will interrupt the beauty of the model with what it costs outside the room.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Let this sound like a seminar between elite Kirin Tor minds: named theorems, competing hypotheses, chalkboard revisions, and half-finished chains Jaina completes a beat before she regrets keeping up.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Kel'Thuzad thinks the completed theory justifies.
What he wants from Jaina beyond simple comprehension.
sensoryAnchors: chalk rasp on slate
elegant notation pushed into forbidden territory
Jaina recognizing the equations are sound before deciding what they mean
beats.transitionIntent: moral-reversal: once the mechanism works, Jaina has to force the conversation back to what the work has done in the world above.
beats.verifierNotes: This beat pays off the partial mechanism trail from chapters 9-10. Treat the theorem as completed here, not as a surprise idea appearing from nowhere.

scenes.verifierModification: Retuned the scene start to honor chapter10's deliberate gap: chapter12 now opens after the stair and initial formalities, with Jaina already inside the sanctum and Jandice only finishing the threshold handoff.

scenes.title: The Duel
scenes.sceneFunction: Shift from mechanism to moral and epistemological combat, use Dalaran's ban to expose the gap between theoretical coherence and ethical acceptability, then let Kel'Thuzad name the structural parallel between his path and Jaina's.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The mechanism is sound enough that Jaina cannot dismiss it as sloppy fraud.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina tries to move from theorem to cost and discovers the theorem still holds.

enrichment.detail: Kel'Thuzad's canon as a former Council of Six archmage expelled over practical necromancy gives the ethics scene sharper footing: he can answer like a dissenter who knows exactly how Dalaran argued the ban because he stood inside that argument. That history supports a calm policy-and-method register instead of villain rhetoric.

beats.goal: Force the conversation from elegant theory back to plague victims, contaminated grain, and mass death.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad does not dodge the moral challenge; he answers it by separating theoretical validity from civic prohibition, arguing that Dalaran banned necromancy because of what it does to persons and institutions, not because the arcane chain fails.

outcome.summary: Jaina cannot simply win by pointing at the dead, and the terms of the argument shift under her feet.

beats.valueShift: rigorous engagement with the mechanism -> epistemological vertigo
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad sees the campaign and the research as one continuous act.
He takes moral challenge seriously enough to answer, not theatrically enough to rant.
The Kirin Tor's analytical tools are good at proving facts and weak at proving value.
Dalaran banned necromancy because its practical use collapses consent, personhood, and civic trust, not because the underlying magical logic is unsound.
The horror is the gradient itself: once low-fidelity preserved function is possible, the step toward preserving the person becomes a question of degree rather than category.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Jaina should invoke the ban as proof of corruption; Kel'Thuzad should answer like a Council dissenter citing policy, precedent, and sound theorems the Council never disproved.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina can find a framework that reaches him.
Whether any moral argument using shared Kirin Tor standards can actually defeat his case.
sensoryAnchors: specimen jars turning from background into accusation
Kel'Thuzad's hands staying still
Jaina's fingers tracing argument in the air
the room remaining clinically ordinary
beats.transitionIntent: framework-shift: Jaina has to change grounds if she wants any purchase at all.

beats.emotion: The ground under the argument is moving.
beats.dilemma: Keep fighting inside Kirin Tor proof-rules that favor him, or shift to consent, personhood, and the human ground those rules were never designed to measure.
beats.decision: Move to consent and coercion; if the mechanism preserves pattern without permission, the violation begins before any elegant theory can save it.
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's best answer is about persons, not purely about models.
Kel'Thuzad acknowledges a strong point when he hears one, which makes the exchange worse rather than easier.
Jaina notices how easily her own body has adopted scholar habits in his room.
Jaina realizes her strongest objection is ethical because the magical framework itself remains horribly coherent.
beats.dialogueGuidance: This is the turn where Jaina stops trying to beat him on theorem alone and starts arguing from consent, selfhood, and the point at which a valid method becomes an atrocity. It is also the turn where she reaches for the human ground Sylvanas would have forced her to price much earlier.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the foothold changes anything beyond the local exchange.
How much of Jaina's resistance depends on frameworks Kel'Thuzad can simply route around.
sensoryAnchors: chalk left in Jaina's hand before she realizes she picked it up
wards humming under the floor
silence used pedagogically rather than mercifully
beats.transitionIntent: personal-mirror: with the framework war exposed, Kel'Thuzad can finally name the pattern running beneath it.

beats.goal: Refute or contain Kel'Thuzad's claim that Jaina followed the same impulse he did.
beats.conflict: The facts support him too well: unauthorized border crossing, contempt for institutional delay, and trust in one's own analysis over sanctioned procedure.

outcome.summary: The parallel cannot be dismissed cleanly, and it reframes Jaina's entire investigation as evidence for the same danger she is trying to deny.

beats.valueShift: precarious foothold -> the mirror is named
newOnPageInformation: Jaina and Kel'Thuzad were both produced by the same institution and broke with it for opposite ends.
The difference between them is direction and cost, not basic method.
Kel'Thuzad is not trying to corrupt her so much as document her as confirming data.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How motive can be defended in a framework that distrusts anything unverifiable.
Whether Jaina can keep the mirror from opening wider once it has been named.
sensoryAnchors: Kirin Tor texts on the shelves reading less like trophies than a still-active library
Kel'Thuzad's gentleness making the claim land harder
Jaina recognizing the pattern before she can reject it
beats.transitionIntent: threshold-crossing: once the mirror is named, the chapter can reach the one heartbeat where Jaina follows the logic too far.

scenes.title: The Mirror
scenes.sceneFunction: Deliver the chapter's core wound — the heartbeat where Jaina sees the structural beauty in Kel'Thuzad's reasoning and cannot unknow that she saw it.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The mirror is named, and Jaina cannot dismiss it without lying to herself.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina's mind can follow the theorem farther than her ethics want to admit.

enrichment.detail: Kel'Thuzad's canon and voice support him as delighted lecturer rather than tormentor, while Jaina's Antonidas-trained profile keeps the mirror sharp: same notation, same institutional discipline, same habit of following evidence until someone else prices the consequence. That shared schooling is the scene's real horror texture.

beats.goal: Resist following Kel'Thuzad's logic past the boundary where understanding becomes dangerous.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad moves from mechanics to implication: preserved neural pattern, continuity of learned structure, and the theoretical gradient between function and self.

outcome.summary: Jaina does not agree with him, and she still follows the logic far enough to see its terrible beauty for one heartbeat.

beats.valueShift: the mirror is named -> comprehension shock
newOnPageInformation: The resonance model implies continuity as gradient, not clean wall.
Jaina's mind can see theoretical beauty in a structure built for atrocity.
Kel'Thuzad notices the precise instant she crosses and recoils.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What she can do with the fact that her mind followed the path at all.
Whether this moment changes anything practical before Chapter 13 breaks the room open.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's hands flattening on the table
a ward ripple through the sanctum
silence dense enough to count as an event
beats.transitionIntent: aftermath-pause: the chapter now has to price that moment rather than rush past it.

beats.dilemma: Deny what happened and call it contamination, or admit that her analytical mind is morally neutral and would have kept going without friction from outside herself.
beats.decision: Sit with the fact honestly and recognize that Sylvanas's absent perspective was part of what kept this from happening earlier.
newOnPageInformation: Understanding is not surrender, but it can still wound.
Jaina now sees the partnership as epistemic friction, not only physical protection.
The same habits that make her good at this work also make this kind of danger possible.
Necromancy is not magically alien to her education; it is a continuous extension of familiar arcane logic, which is exactly why the ban had to be moral rather than mathematical.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How she keeps investigating without letting the same lens run unchecked again.
How any institution would distinguish understanding from agreement if she reported this exactly.
sensoryAnchors: breath discipline used for composure
the room's banality turning worse because it does not react
research notes lying on the desk as if none of this were personal
beats.transitionIntent: mentor-wound: Kel'Thuzad now turns the wound toward Antonidas and makes the mirror intimate rather than abstract.
beats.verifierNotes: Sylvanas remains off-page in this chapter until the final beat. Her presence here is remembered friction, not live external observation.

beats.goal: Test the shared-teacher wound and expose how institutional caution shaped both Jaina and Kel'Thuzad.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad's assessment of Antonidas is accurate enough to hurt: generous teacher, limited thinker, unable to follow where his own best students went.

outcome.summary: Jaina gains sharper language for the institutional failure, but the language also implicates the mentor she still loves.

newOnPageInformation: Antonidas trained both Jaina and Kel'Thuzad in the same intellectual tradition.
Institutional caution helped leave the kingdom analytically unprepared for necromantic war.
Jaina's anger at Dalaran and fear of herself can coexist without cancelling each other.
Antonidas and the Council banned practical necromancy because once the theory touches real bodies, the line between scholarship and violation collapses.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Let Kel'Thuzad frame the ban as Dalaran losing its nerve at the point where sound theory touched real bodies; Jaina should hear how seductive that argument is even while rejecting its conclusion.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What she can safely tell Antonidas later without being treated as contaminated by the exchange.
Whether Antonidas's caution was failure, mercy, or both.
sensoryAnchors: warmth entering Kel'Thuzad's voice only when he says Antonidas's name
Antonidas's notation system visible on every surface
Jaina feeling the teacher present in both the monster's handwriting and her own habits
beats.transitionIntent: scale-widening: once the personal wound is open, Kel'Thuzad can widen the frame from one institution's failure to the real scale of the war.
beats.verifierNotes: Antonidas is present only through shared training, remembered doctrine, and prior stone-line contact. He is not a live participant in the room.

scenes.title: The Revelation
scenes.sceneFunction: Expand the threat from plague mechanism to cosmic logistics, then show that Kel'Thuzad has already priced his own death into the system.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina now understands both the mechanism and the personal mirror; the room is ready for scale.
scenes.arcPressure: Each answer widens the war until intellect stops feeling like protection.

enrichment.detail: Canonical chain-of-command grounding supports Kel'Thuzad naming the Lich King as the immediate patron power and the Burning Legion as the larger invasion frame without dumping full cosmology. That keeps the disclosure in strategic-briefing register: enough to widen the war, not enough to resolve it.

beats.goal: Learn who or what sits above Kel'Thuzad and why the plague exists at all.
beats.conflict: The answer is bigger than rogue scholarship: a patron-intelligence in Northrend that found Kel'Thuzad before his expulsion and accelerated work the Kirin Tor would never sanction.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets the first classified layer of the real architecture, but the scale instantly outruns the frame she came in with.

newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad names the Lich King as a bound intelligence in Northrend rather than a simple necromancer.
Kel'Thuzad sees the relationship as patron-scholar, not master-servant.
The plague is logistics, not merely experiment.
Kel'Thuzad did not derive the whole design in isolation; Ner'zhul supplied theory, revisions, and patron knowledge beyond anything Dalaran could have given him.
beats.disclosureProvenance: Kel'Thuzad is the first and only on-page source for the Lich King here; Jaina does not already have the term from Dalaran-side knowledge.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What created the Lich King.
Whether anything above Kel'Thuzad and the Lich King is directing the preparation.
sensoryAnchors: older colder ward signatures in the walls
Kel'Thuzad sounding grateful rather than afraid when he describes the voice
Jaina feeling the room as instrument rather than shelter
beats.transitionIntent: top-of-chain: the patron now points to its own creator, and the frame widens one more time.
beats.verifierNotes: First on-page naming of the Lich King. Jaina does not begin the chapter already carrying this term or its chain-of-command meaning.

beats.goal: Understand the customer for the infrastructure Kel'Thuzad built.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad widens the frame by naming the force above the Lich King, but he does it as strategic briefing rather than exhaustive cosmology, leaving Jaina with scale before she gets detail.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets the full strategic picture, but the picture is so large that it reveals how structurally insufficient her current tools are.

newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad names the Burning Legion as the force above the Lich King.
The Scourge is preparation for invasion, not the final threat.
Jaina gets the name and strategic frame, not a full cosmology lecture.
Some plague side effects and downstream behaviors may belong to the design stack above Kel'Thuzad rather than to anything he fully modeled end-to-end himself.
beats.disclosureProvenance: Kel'Thuzad is also the first on-page source for the Burning Legion; it enters as a brief top-of-chain namedrop, not as ambient Dalaran knowledge or a full lore dump.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Which current institutions, if any, can respond to a threat at this scale.
Whether the countermeasure she can now design will matter in time.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's fingers whitening in her lap
specimen jars shrinking into insignificance beside what they now imply
Kel'Thuzad's voice flattening into briefing register
beats.transitionIntent: mortality-price: once the scale is fully visible, the chapter only needs the final mirror — what Kel'Thuzad thinks his own death means inside it.
beats.verifierNotes: First on-page naming of the Burning Legion. Keep it as Kel'Thuzad's disclosure, not ambient shared lore.

beats.emotion: The calm of a concluded proof.
beats.dilemma: Argue with a man who has already priced his own death into the continuity of his work, or finally stop debating and understand what actually separates her from him.
beats.decision: Stop arguing and register the real difference: Kel'Thuzad has a patron who will preserve his usefulness; Jaina has a partner who would come for her.
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad expects resurrection as a lich and treats it as solved continuity rather than sacrifice.
His attachment is to scholarship and continuation of method, not to mortal life as such.
He treats the village plague's crude choir as proof-of-concept, not as the preservation standard he wants for himself.
Jaina's real separation from him is relational context and value, not mere intelligence.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether she can get out with the data before the room changes from seminar back into battlefield.
Whether Sylvanas's arrival will heal the absence or wound it further.
sensoryAnchors: Kel'Thuzad discussing his own death with no more heat than harmonic notation
sanctum light dimming as the wards cycle colder
Jaina thinking of Sylvanas not as theory but as the person who would come
beats.transitionIntent: silence-weight: the conversation is functionally over; what remains is the silence it leaves behind and the disturbance coming through it.

scenes.title: The Silence and the Sound
scenes.sceneFunction: Let the chapter settle the weight of what Jaina learned, then break the sanctum's stillness with the first sign of Sylvanas's approach.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina has everything she came for and one wound she cannot file away.

enrichment.detail: Caer Darrow's island keep and Scholomance's layered crypt structure support the final approach registering as wards, pressure shifts, and building-scale response before visible entry. Using layered defensive ripples rather than direct sight keeps the close simultaneous with Chapter 11's northbound redirect and ready for Chapter 13's arrival beat.

beats.dilemma: Move immediately on incomplete internal processing, or sit one beat longer and understand what the conversation actually did to her before the next action begins.
beats.decision: Stay in the silence long enough for the weight to settle rather than pretending it has not changed her.
beats.valueShift: the mirror completes -> the weight settles
newOnPageInformation: Jaina now has the complete mechanism, the cosmic frame, and the knowledge of her own dangerous susceptibility to elegant logic.
She now understands necromancy as continuous with accepted arcane knowledge rather than as a magically disconnected profanation.
Kel'Thuzad is satisfied because he was understood, not because he won a theatrical contest.
The room remains offensively ordinary despite what has happened inside it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How any outsider will interpret this scene when they arrive.
Whether Jaina can separate what she learned from what she felt about the learning.
sensoryAnchors: silence deep enough to feel physical
the research log from Chapter 10 sitting on the desk as if it belonged there all along
Jaina sitting back not in relief but in receded shock
beats.transitionIntent: ward-answer: the stillness breaks because the building starts answering someone else's movement.
beats.verifierNotes: The room's offensive ordinariness is a callback to chapter10's anti-Dalaran institution, not a new reveal of place.

beats.goal: Register the outside approach and bridge directly into Chapter 13.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad notices the disturbance first through the building's ward architecture; Jaina feels it a beat later and knows the movement pattern before she can name it aloud.

outcome.summary: The rescue is coming, but it will arrive too late to prevent the moment and early enough to misread its aftermath.

newOnPageInformation: Someone is moving through Scholomance with tactical, physical precision rather than magical blundering.
The ward cascades describe a descent from the upper levels toward the sanctum.
Jaina recognizes the approach as Sylvanas before the name is spoken.
Kel'Thuzad reads the change on Jaina's face but does not understand the partnership behind it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How Sylvanas will interpret the tableau when she arrives.
Whether Chapter 13 opens on rescue, confrontation, or both at once.
sensoryAnchors: defensive wards activating in layered ripples
no visible spellcraft attached to the approach
relentless room-clearing motion
Kel'Thuzad's attention redirecting upward with amused curiosity
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-entry: Chapter 13 begins at entry — Sylvanas in the sanctum, seeing the aftermath without the context.
beats.verifierNotes: Only Sylvanas reaches sanctum depth here; Cyndia remains on the approach and outside the room. Recognition of the approach stays inside Jaina's perception.

warnings: Scene-beat outcomes are narrow: beats 1, 2, 8, 9, 10, and 13 land yes-but; beats 3, 5, and 6 land no-and; there is no clean yes. The prose will need sharp cost differentiation so the chapter's wins and losses do not flatten together.

Scenes 1-4 keep the same room, mostly the same cast, and sustained dialogue/theory combat. The mechanism -> ethics -> mirror -> cosmic-scale progression has to change the kind of pressure each time or the middle will blur into one long seminar.

Beats 8-10 escalate in the same darkening direction — mentor wound, Lich King reveal, Burning Legion reveal — so the late middle risks monotony unless each beat lands as a different category of injury rather than one repeated awe-horror note.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter10's controlled descent: after Jandice brings Jaina below on the master's permission, chapter12 opens after the stair and initial formalities, with Jaina already inside Kel'Thuzad's sanctum and the theoretical exchange underway. Chapter11 runs in simultaneous exterior time only; Jaina has no knowledge of Arthas's mobilization or Sylvanas's northbound turn until the final ward disturbance announces an approach she recognizes.

craftResearch.findings: Keep the exchange powered by opposed goals, subtext, and power shifts rather than information transfer alone. Silence, precise physical beats, and reversals keep an intellectual duel from going static.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: dialogue-conflict craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: A same-room chapter needs clear changing goals, escalating stakes, and visible turning points so the pressure type keeps changing even when the walls do not.
Interiority and sequel beats must price the fallout of each escalation before the next turn lands, or the chapter flattens into one uninterrupted note.
Dialogue scenes stay alive when body language, room details, and sensory focus shift with the argument, turning setting into an active tension surface instead of static wallpaper.
End on unresolved consequence or incoming disruption, not on settled reflection. The reveal should widen the next danger and force the reader into the next chapter.

pacing.notes: The chapter rises in distinct bands: room-legibility, mechanism, ethics, mirror, scale, aftermath, threshold. Sequel beats 4, 7, 11, and 12 give real breathing room after the harder spikes at 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, and 13. The same-room risk is real, but the pressure type changes often enough to keep the middle from reading as one long seminar.

structure.notes: The opening seam needed correction: chapter10 promised a deliberate gap, so beat 1 now opens after the stair and formalities instead of replaying arrival. The mid-chapter turn lands cleanly when the argument stops being about method and becomes self-indictment at beats 5-6. The close does what the chapter must do: it leaves Jaina with the answer, the wound, and an incoming witness who lacks context.

character.notes: Jaina stays active in every scene: she redirects the argument, changes frameworks, accepts the moral wound instead of denying it, and chooses stillness deliberately once the conversation ends. Kel'Thuzad remains distinct from stock villainy by staying calm, lucid, and pedagogical. Jandice remains threshold-only as promised, and Sylvanas is held off-page until the final beat as missing friction rather than recap.

transitions.notes: All 13 beat-level transitionIntent chains are present and coherent. No beat-level intents needed to be added. One chapter-seam intentional break from chapter10 is now explicitly governed by the chapterBridge, and beat 13 now carries a guard that only Sylvanas reaches sanctum depth while Cyndia remains outside the room.

continuity.notes: 11 beats evolve established material and 2 beats introduce first-time top-chain disclosures: the Lich King and the Burning Legion. Fixed 1 seam contradiction by honoring chapter10's promised gap at the opening. Added continuity guards against reintroducing Scholomance and Jandice as brand-new gothic spectacle and against treating the Lich King / Burning Legion names as already-known lore.

modifications.description: Retuned scene 1 and beat 1 so chapter12 opens after chapter10's deliberate descent gap, with Jaina already inside the sanctum and Jandice only finishing the threshold handoff.
Tagged beats 9 and 10 as new first disclosures for the Lich King and the Burning Legion instead of treating those names as preloaded lore.
Added end-state continuity guard that only Sylvanas reaches sanctum depth at beat 13 while Cyndia remains outside, preserving the chapter11 northbound geometry for chapter13.
